title: Suez Canal Post-Ceasefire: Outlook for Capesize Transits
section_path: Suez Canal Post-Ceasefire: Outlook for Capesize Transits
section_path_text: Suez Canal Post-Ceasefire: Outlook for Capesize Transits
summary:
Main
Image reference: RGB_COLOR_BRS_SHIPBROKERS_MONOGRAM_LOGO.png | assets/2025-12-09_suez-canal-post-ceasefire-outlook-for-capesize-transits_img_rgb-color-brs-shipbrokers-monogram-l_c365f35f1de5.png
The Red Sea crisis, now stretching into its third year, has shown a sudden turning point. On 11 November, Yemen's Houthi rebels issued a statement announcing that it had stopped attacks against Israel and commercial shipping in the Red Sea. This statement marked the first time that the Houthis had formally confirmed a suspension of attacks since the Gaza ceasefire agreement came into effect on 10 October.
